latest.title: Extension of Tungsten Project Area
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 9914W
Great Western Mining Corp. plc
18 March 2026
Great Western Mining Corporation PLC / AIM: GWMO / Euronext Growth: 8GW
18 March 2026
GREAT WESTERN MINING CORPORATION PLC
("Great Western", "GWM" or the "Company")
Extension of Tungsten Project Area
Great Western Mining Corporation PLC, the AIM-quoted exploration and development company focused on mineral projects in the state of Nevada, USA, is pleased to announce that it has staked an additional 165.3 acres of claims contiguous to the Company's Defender-Pine Crow Tungsten Project in Mineral County, Nevada ("Defender-Pine Crow" or the "Project").
The expansion of its land package at Defender-Pine Crow consolidates the Company's footprint in an area which has demonstrated prospectivity for high-grade tungsten mineralisation.
·
Eight new claim blocks, covering 165.3 acres (67 hectares), staked in Mineral County, Nevada and filed with both the county and with the federal Bureau of Land Management ("BLM").
·
Extends the northern and eastern boundary of the Company's existing claim holdings in the Black Mountains.
·
Provides an expanded buffer zone around the confirmed tungsten occurrences, which are a focus of the Company's current exploration activities.
Great Western CEO Ed Loye commented:
"We are pleased to have secured these additional claim blocks at Defender-Pine Crow, further strengthening our land position in what is shaping up to be a very prospective tungsten district in Nevada. Results from the Project to date have been highly encouraging and the exploration team is excited about the potential we are seeing on the ground.
"Expanding our footprint in this area is therefore a natural step and an important part of our wider exploration strategy for 2026, which is focused on advancing Defender-Pine Crow towards a maiden Mineral Resource Estimate by the end of the year. These new claims not only extend our coverage to the north and east but also provide a wider operational buffer around the historic Defender workings.
"With winter conditions receding, our team will be back on site in the next few weeks to continue to implement this year's fully funded exploration programme and we are all eager to begin work and start feeding results back to the market. Encouraging results have already been achieved and with an expanded land package now in place, we look forward to building momentum at Defender-Pine Crow throughout the coming field season."
**ENDS**
For further information visit
www.greatwesternmining.com
or contact:

Notes
Great Western Mining Corporation PLC
(AIM: GWMO / Euronext Growth: 8GW) is a diversified exploration and development company focused on strategic minerals across several 100%-owned claim groups in Mineral County, Nevada, one of the world's most prolific and mining-friendly jurisdictions. Backed by an experienced team, the Company is pursuing a multi-commodity strategy targeting near-term development and longer term exploration while assessing farm-out and joint venture opportunities to unlock broader portfolio value. As well as its flagship Huntoon Copper Project, which hosts a JORC-compliant resource of 4.3 Mt at 0.45% Cu, with upside from undrilled porphyry targets, it has a pipeline of gold, silver, and early-stage tungsten assets aligned with US critical minerals priorities.
Forward‑Looking Statements
This announcement contains forward‑looking statements relating to the plans, activities and expectations of Great Western Mining Corporation PLC. Such statements include, but are not limited to, those concerning planned exploration programmes, anticipated timelines and potential mineral resource outcomes. Forward‑looking statements are typically identified by words such as "plan", "expect", "anticipate", "intend", "may", "could", "potential" or similar expressions. These statements are based on current expectations and assumptions and involve risks and uncertainties that could cause actual results to differ materially. Factors include geological risk, exploration and drilling results, permitting and regulatory approvals, funding availability, operational challenges, commodity price movements and general market conditions. No assurance can be given that any forward‑looking statements will prove to be accurate, and shareholders are cautioned not to place undue reliance on them.

latest.title: Grant of Share Options
latest.content:
Today 11:35
RNS Number : 6209W
Great Western Mining Corp. plc
13 March 2026
Great Western Mining Corporation PLC / AIM: GWMO / Euronext Growth: 8GW
13 March 2026
GREAT WESTERN MINING CORPORATION PLC
("Great Western", "GWM" or the "Company")
Grant of Share Options
Great Western Mining Corporation PLC, a strategic minerals exploration and development company, announced that it has granted options over 4,000,000 ordinary shares of €0.0001 each in the capital of the Company to Ed Loye, Chief Executive Officer of Great Western.
The options have an exercise price of 1.69 pence per ordinary share and have been granted pursuant to the terms of Mr Loye's employment agreement.
The options are exercisable in accordance with the terms of the Company's share option scheme.

latest.title: Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
Today 15:51
RNS Number : 9914U
Great Western Mining Corp. plc
02 March 2026
Great Western Mining Corporation PLC / AIM: GWMO / Euronext Growth: 8GW
2 March 2026
GREAT WESTERN MINING CORPORATION PLC
("Great Western", "GWM" or the "Company")
Total Voting Rights
In conformity with Regulation 20 of the Transparency (Directive 2004/109/EC) Regulations 2007 and following the recent Share Capital Reorganisation, Great Western announces that as at 2 March 2026 the total number of Ordinary Shares of ?0.0001 each in issue (with voting rights) is 411,832,131. The Company does not hold any ordinary shares in treasury.
This figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in Great Western under the Transparency (Directive 2004/109/EC) Regulations 2007 and the Transparency Rules.

company_30s.what_they_do: GWMO is a small, UK‑listed mining group that focuses on tungsten exploration and development.  They target high‑grade tungsten deposits and plan to bring them into production once sufficient resources and funding are secured.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Tungsten is a strategic metal used in hard‑metal tools, armor‑piercing munitions and high‑temperature alloys.  Global demand is tied to defence budgets, automotive and aerospace manufacturing, and the push for high‑strength, lightweight materials.  GWMO’s projects sit in mineral‑rich regions that could fill supply gaps if the company can unlock the resources.
company_30s.current_state: The company is in an early, high‑risk phase.  It has recently raised capital through a £3.25 m placing to fund exploration, and it is actively drilling and surveying new areas.  Management is still building a track record, so cash burn is high and profitability is far off.  The share price is extremely low and volatile, reflecting the speculative nature of the business.

rns_narrative: The company is expanding its tungsten footprint – an extension of the project area and new drilling results at the Rhyolite Dome suggest additional resources.
Governance moves – a new CEO and share‑option grant – signal an effort to bring in fresh leadership and align insiders with long‑term upside.
Capital structure updates – the recent placing and the grant of voting rights indicate the board is actively managing ownership and liquidity to support the exploration push.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Skeptical chatter with a sprinkling of optimism around the latest drilling
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders are bullish, citing the 557 % tungsten price jump and the company’s recent mineralisation confirmation as signs that a market‑wide supply shock could lift the stock.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Many observers warn that the company has no production history, high cash burn and a very thin share base.  They fear the stock could remain trapped in a low‑price zone until a major catalyst materialises.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Over the past 24 hours the tone has stayed flat – no new headlines or analyst upgrades have shifted the narrative.  The community is largely waiting for a breakthrough announcement or a price move.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no recent interest, meaning the market is largely unaware of GWMO’s story.

near_term_catalysts: A formal resource estimate that upgrades the deposit grade or size would give investors confidence.
An exploration‑stage completion report that shows a clear path to production and a realistic cost estimate.
A strategic partnership or JV announcement with a larger mining player that could provide funding or technical expertise.
A significant jump in tungsten prices (e.g., above $70/oz) that would turn the company’s resource into a lucrative play.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden spike in trading volume accompanied by a price break above the recent 20‑day moving average.  A news release that confirms a new resource estimate or a partnership would also be a key trigger.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a potential crash‑recovery cycle – the price has dipped sharply but has not yet found a firm support level.  If the APEX score is high (tighter compression) and volume is still thin, a mean‑reversion bounce could be imminent once a catalyst arrives.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Contrarian Bullish – the deep crash and dead market awareness create a surprise opportunity if a catalyst materialises.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the stock rebounds to the halfway point between its current level and the peak seen in 2025, the upside would be roughly 200‑300 % from today’s price.  The signal hierarchy suggests that a deep crash (Green or Orange) typically delivers 39‑112 % returns, so a 50‑70 % recovery would still be a substantial win.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company still faces a long, capital‑intensive path to production.  Without a clear resource upgrade or partnership, the price could stay stuck in the low‑penny range for years.  A realistic downside would be a 60‑80 % drop if the stock fails to attract new capital or if tungsten prices fall.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 200‑300 % if the price recovers to halfway levels.  Potential downside: 70‑80 % from the current low.  The asymmetry is favourable for a contrarian long because the upside is much larger than the risk.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a break of the 20‑day moving average with a minimum 10‑day volume spike.  Size the position so that a 30 % loss would not hurt your overall portfolio – for example, 1‑2 % of your capital per trade.  Place a stop 10 % below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see either a new resource estimate or a partnership announcement.  Also watch for a sustained rise in tungsten prices.  Once either occurs, look for a small pullback to re‑enter at a better price.
playbook.if_passing: None – there are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy risks.  The only terminal risk is a prolonged lack of catalysts, which would keep the stock in a low‑price zone.

ai_insights.bottom_line: GWMO’s deep crash and silent market create a high‑reward, low‑risk contrarian play that will only materialise once a clear resource or partnership catalyst appears.
